parties.display_name: Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC / BMO Capital Markets Corp.

cohorts.label: potential strategic buyers contacted during week of March 28, 2016
potential financial buyers contacted during week of March 28, 2016
potential strategic buyers that executed confidentiality agreements
potential financial buyers that executed confidentiality agreements
potential buyers that submitted written indications of interest between May 19 and June 1, 2016
remaining seven potential buyers invited to management presentations after two low bidders excluded
parties that submitted LOIs in late July 2016

observations.summary: In Q4 2015, Company management met with Party A, a Class I rail partner, who expressed interest in acquiring equity in the Company.
observations.other_detail: initial meeting between Company management and Party A discussing joint commercial opportunities

observations.summary: Upon receiving report of Party A meeting, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors to interview potential investment bankers.
observations.other_detail: Board appointed subcommittee to interview investment bankers

observations.summary: At the January 27, 2016 Board meeting, the subcommittee recommended retaining GHF as investment banking firm. The Board approved and the Company engaged GHF.

At a special Board meeting on March 14, 2016, GHF proposed a process to proceed with Party A discussions and solicit interest from other third parties regarding a change in control transaction. The Board concluded it was in the best interest of the Company and its shareholders to proceed.

At the conclusion of the March 14, 2016 meeting, the Board appointed a Transaction Committee composed of Frank W. Barrett (Chair), Richard W. Anderson, and James C. Garvey to supervise and oversee the process.
During the week of March 28, 2016, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers.
Each of the 11 potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements. The agreements did not contain any standstill or similar provision.
On March 22-23, 2016, the Company's CFO and senior management met with various representatives of Party A.
Between April 3-6, 2016, representatives of GHF and Company management held introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers (including G&W) at the 2016 Connections Convention.
On April 21, 2016, the Company and GHF held an introductory meeting with Party B, another potential strategic buyer.
On April 27, 2016, the Board discussed the process status, including that each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding IOI by May 10, 2016 (subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016).

Between May 19 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written IOIs from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50, implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
The Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from the management presentation process.
The Transaction Committee authorized GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow them to conduct additional due diligence.
In mid-June 2016, GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding LOIs by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of merger agreement, disclosure letter, and voting agreement forms.
On June 29, 2016, the Company posted an initial draft of merger agreement to the data site; on June 30, 2016, a draft voting agreement; on July 11, 2016, a draft disclosure letter.
observations.other_detail: draft merger agreement posted to data site

observations.summary: In early July 2016, Party C, a potential strategic buyer not previously part of the process, approached the Company's management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.
Party C executed a confidentiality agreement and was provided the Company memorandum.
On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying equity value of $108 million).

In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96M to $121M). Party B, G&W, and Party D provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement. Party E provided a summary of material issues.
Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for $24.00 per share, subject to an expedited diligence review.
G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 for $21.15 per share, subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period. The price included $20.02 cash at closing plus a CVR.
Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI for $21.26 per share, subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation.
Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI for $21.00 per share, subject to a four-week diligence period.
Party C submitted an LOI for $19.30 per share, subject to a 30-day diligence period.
Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI for $19.20 per share, subject to a 30-day due diligence period.
One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.

The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers relative to other potential buyers.
At the direction of the Transaction Committee, GHF contacted the remaining bidders to inform them they were no longer involved in the process.
On July 29, 2016, Party D and Party E both expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing their offers during calls with GHF.
On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share with a 30-day due diligence period.
On August 1, 2016, Party E submitted a revised LOI at $23.81 per share, along with financing support from Party F, with a 30-day due diligence period.
On August 2, 2016, Party E withdrew its revised proposal of $23.81 per share but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share.
observations.other_detail: withdrew revised proposal but confirmed original proposal

observations.summary: Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested exclusivity. The Company declined, at which point Party D indicated it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
On August 4, 2016, the Transaction Committee directed the team to continue diligence and negotiations with both G&W and Party B, but to prioritize Party B given the higher price offered by Party B.
observations.other_detail: Transaction Committee directed to prioritize Party B for merger agreement negotiation

observations.summary: On August 9, 2016, the Transaction Committee directed BMO to inform G&W that the Company intended to enter into a merger agreement with another bidder. G&W requested to continue due diligence to facilitate an improved offer, which was granted.
observations.other_detail: Transaction Committee directed BMO to inform G&W that Company intended to enter into merger agreement with another bidder

observations.summary: On August 11, 2016, G&W indicated its possible intention to submit a revised LOI on August 12, 2016, having just completed its physical diligence.

On the morning of August 12, 2016, G&W submitted a revised LOI for $25.00 per share in cash (excluding the previously proposed CVR), together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and voting agreement. The offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. the following day.
On August 12, 2016, the Transaction Committee directed BMO to inform Party B that another party had submitted a revised LOI at a higher price.
observations.other_detail: Party B informed that another party submitted revised LOI at higher price

observations.summary: BMO contacted Party B to determine if it would increase its offer price. Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.

On August 12, 2016, the Board determined that the G&W offer represented the superior proposal. BMO rendered an oral opinion (subsequently confirmed in writing) that the merger consideration was fair from a financial point of view to the holders of common stock.
observations.other_detail: Board determined G&W offer was superior proposal; BMO rendered fairness opinion

observations.summary: On August 12, 2016, the Board determined the merger agreement was fair, advisable and in the best interests of the Company and its shareholders, approved the merger agreement and transactions, directed submission to shareholders for approval, and resolved to recommend shareholder approval.
Shortly after Board approval on August 12, 2016, the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement.
The Company and G&W executed the merger agreement on August 12, 2016.
On August 15, 2016, the Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction prior to the opening of trading on NASDAQ.

On September 6, 2016, the Board considered BMO's disclosure that it had failed to identify a BMO affiliate's participation in G&W's syndicated debt facility, determined this was not material to BMO's independence, and confirmed comfort with BMO's engagement and opinion.
observations.other_detail: Board considered BMO conflict disclosure and confirmed independence

exclusions.explanation: Fairness opinion financial analyses detail: The filing references BMO's financial analyses supporting its opinion but the detailed analyses are described elsewhere in the proxy. Only the opinion delivery event is extracted.

Eder Trusts voting agreement terms: The voting agreement with the Eder Trusts is mentioned as executed alongside the merger agreement but specific terms are not detailed in the Background of the Merger section.

Director Smith/GATX recusal details: Director Smith's recusal is noted as context. GATX's 4.92% ownership and the possibility it might be a potential buyer are disclosed but no GATX bid or participation in the process is described.

STB regulatory approval process details: The Board's discussion of STB approval processes for G&W versus Party B is referenced but constitutes regulatory analysis rather than an extractable M&A observation.

Termination fee terms: The filing describes termination fee provisions (3% for G&W, 3% plus expenses capped at 1% for Party B) as part of draft merger agreement comparison. These are agreement terms noted in context but not separate agreement observations since they describe proposed terms within draft agreements under negotiation.
